concerns.text: Task T017 (Implement stratified splitting logic) is placed in Phase 4 (US2) but is a prerequisite for T018 (Training loop). The training loop (T018) consumes the train/val/test splits generated by T017. If T018 runs before T017, it has no data to train on. T017 must precede T018 within the execution order, even if they are in the same phase.

Tasks T023 (SHAP) and T024 (Permutation importance) are marked [P] (parallel-safe) and placed before T025 (Composition-only baseline). However, T023/T024 require a *trained* GNN model to compute feature importance. T025 defines the baseline model, but the *training* of the main GNN (T018) and the *training* of the baseline (T025) must complete before T023/T024 can run. The current ordering implies T023/T024 can run in parallel with T025, but they actually depend on the output of the training tasks (T018/T025). T023/T024 must be ordered AFTER the training tasks they consume.

Tasks T029-T031 (Revision & Compliance) are placed in Phase 6, dependent on 'all user stories being implemented'. However, T029 updates `download.py` (US1), T030 updates `train.py` (US2), and T031 updates `report_generator.py` (US3). These are code modifications to the core implementation tasks. Placing them in a separate 'Revision' phase after implementation creates a semantic dependency violation: the 'implementation' of the user stories is incomplete without these specific compliance fixes (terminology corrections). The tasks should be integrated into their respective US phases (T029 in US1, T030 in US2, T031 in US3) or the US phases must explicitly depend on the completion of these specific compliance tasks before the story is considered 'done'.

Task T013 (Create data pipeline orchestration script) is listed after T009-T012. While logically the pipeline orchestrates the previous tasks, the description 'download -> parse -> filter -> save' implies it calls the functions in T009, T010, T011. If T013 is implemented before T009-T012 are complete, it will fail. The [P] tag is missing on T009-T012, but T013 clearly depends on the *completion* of T009-T012. The ordering is correct (T013 after T009-T012), but the 'Parallel Opportunities' section claims 'All Setup tasks marked [P] can run in parallel' and 'All Foundational tasks marked [P] can run in parallel'. T009-T012 are not marked [P], which is correct, but the text 'Once Foundational phase completes, all user stories can start in parallel' is misleading if T013 (US1) depends on T009-T012 (US1) which are not parallel with each other. The text should clarify that US1 tasks have internal dependencies.
